trade.summary: Substantial trade under USMCA (formerly CUSMA in Canada). Canada exports crude oil, canola, machinery, and automotive parts to Mexico. Mexico exports vehicles, electronics, auto parts, produce, and manufactured goods to Canada. Mexico runs a significant trade surplus.
disputes: Periodic disputes over agricultural products and labor standards under USMCA

diplomatic.summary: Canada and Mexico are natural partners in managing the USMCA relationship with the US. Both have coordinated positions on USMCA renegotiations and share interest in preserving the trilateral trade framework. Relations are friendly though less intense than either country's relationship with the US.

military.summary: No formal military alliance beyond broad North American defense cooperation (NORAD is US-Canada only). Some cooperation on transnational crime and security. Canada has development security programs in Mexico.

financial.summary: Canadian companies and pension funds invest significantly in Mexico (mining, energy, financial services). Scotiabank has major Mexican operations. Mexican companies have growing Canadian presence.

energy.summary: Canada exports oil to Mexico and has energy investment interests. Canadian companies have participated in Mexico's energy sector reforms (and partial reversals). Growing cooperation on clean energy.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation facilitated by USMCA research provisions. Collaboration in environment, health, and agriculture. NSERC-CONACYT programs support joint research.
